title: Google Vertex AI: Managed GenAI Inference
description: Managed GenAI inference on Google Cloud Vertex AI with Gemini, Imagen, and embedding models; strong multimodal support; GCP IAM and regional controls; suitable when AI inference should stay inside a GCP-governed architecture.

cost.costNotes: Managed GenAI pricing varies by model family, token volume, and modality. Track prompt/response tokens, image generation volume, and regional usage to control spend. Use model tiering and caching to avoid overpaying for simple requests.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Pay-as-you-go model inference pricing on Vertex AI creates highly variable monthly cost. Min ($10): light prototyping or internal tooling with low request volume using Gemini Flash or embeddings. Max ($12,000): production workloads with sustained multimodal inference, large-context prompts, or high image generation volume using premium Gemini/Imagen tiers. Typical production assistant or document-analysis workload with moderate traffic can range from $100-800/month depending on token volume and model choice.
provenance.adoptionCost: Low-to-medium integration complexity. Basic Vertex AI SDK or REST integration is straightforward for teams already on GCP, but production readiness requires: service account/IAM setup, quota management, region selection, monitoring, retry logic, and cost controls. Teams also need to understand model routing, context limits, and provider-specific auth. Basic integration: 4-8 hours. Production hardening and observability: additional 6-10 hours. Total: 10-18 hours at typical cloud-platform engineering rates.
provenance.licenseCost: No separate software license fee. Vertex AI uses consumption-based billing tied to model inference usage, optional managed features, and standard GCP billing/account setup. Enterprise commitments may exist at the GCP contract level, but there is no standalone Vertex AI license purchase required for basic API use.

provides.reasoning: Core capability - this pattern provides managed generative AI inference through Google Vertex AI.
Gemini models on Vertex AI provide primary text generation and transformation capabilities for prompts, summaries, and structured outputs.
Vertex AI supports conversational and multi-turn interaction patterns through Gemini chat-style inference.
Vertex AI provides managed image generation through Imagen models, making image generation a first-class capability.
Vertex AI provides managed text embedding models for retrieval, semantic search, and similarity workloads.
Dedicated embedding endpoints generate vector representations directly from raw text and multimodal inputs where supported.
Gemini models support multimodal inputs and outputs, enabling text-plus-image reasoning and generation workflows.
Gemini on Vertex AI is well-suited to document summarisation, extraction, and multimodal analysis workloads operating inside GCP.
Gemini models support structured tool or function invocation for agentic and orchestration scenarios.

requires.reasoning: Vertex AI inference is exposed through managed APIs and SDK-backed HTTP requests.
Vertex AI is tightly coupled to Google Cloud IAM, project scoping, and permission boundaries.
Production access typically relies on GCP service accounts or workload identity rather than anonymous or simple static auth.
Managed GenAI billing varies significantly by model family, token volume, and modality; explicit cost monitoring is needed.
Vertex AI enforces quotas and rate limits that must be respected to avoid throttling or quota exhaustion.
Model inference requests can fail due to transient service issues, quota exhaustion, or malformed requests and need robust retry/error handling.

availability.reason: Pattern for a publicly available managed Google Cloud service.

supports_nfr.reason: Vertex AI runs on managed Google Cloud infrastructure and can support high availability expectations within regional managed-service limits.
Managed GenAI inference on Vertex AI still requires network transit and model execution time, so sub-300ms p95 targets are unrealistic.
Tail latency includes model queueing, multimodal processing, and managed-service variance; p99 below 800ms is not a safe planning assumption.
Managed Google Cloud services can typically recover within minutes through platform failover and operational automation.
Enterprise Vertex AI quotas can support large-scale throughput, but capacity is still bounded by per-project quotas and model-specific limits.
GDPR support depends on region selection, retention configuration, and application-layer controls, not on the provider pattern alone.
CCPA support depends on data handling and deletion controls implemented around the managed service.
HIPAA support depends on covered-service scope, BAA status, and application controls; this provider pattern alone does not guarantee it.
SOX support depends on audit logging, change controls, and environment governance implemented around the managed service.

supports_constraints.reason: Vertex AI is a Google Cloud managed service and should be selected only when the architecture is explicitly GCP-based.
Vertex AI is accessible through REST APIs and SDKs for major languages, with strongest first-party support for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Java, and Go.
Vertex AI can be called from any application or service platform able to use Google-authenticated API access.
Pattern is specifically for AI inference workloads and should only match when that feature is enabled.

primary_model.description: Primary Vertex AI model. gemini-2.5-pro: highest quality for complex reasoning; gemini-2.5-flash: recommended default for balanced latency/cost; gemini-2.0-flash: lower-latency general use; gemini-2.0-flash-lite: cheapest fallback for simple tasks.

fallback_model.description: Fallback model if the primary model is unavailable or quota-limited.

image_model.description: Managed image generation model. Use none if the workload is text-only.

max_tokens.description: Maximum generated tokens per response. Higher values increase latency and spend.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature controlling determinism versus creativity.

enable_streaming.description: Stream partial responses for interactive UX when supported by the selected model.

timeout_seconds.description: Request timeout in seconds for inference calls.

retry_strategy.description: Retry strategy for transient Vertex AI failures or quota throttling.

service_auth_method.description: Service credential method for Vertex AI. Use Google Cloud service account or workload identity, not static provider keys embedded in code.

warn_nfr.reason: Very low-volume usage may not justify the extra project, IAM, and quota setup overhead of Vertex AI compared with simpler external APIs.
warn_nfr.message: genai-inference--vertex-ai: peak_jobs_per_hour is {actual} (<10). Vertex AI setup and governance overhead may outweigh the benefit at very low volume.

warn_nfr.reason: PII workloads on Vertex AI require explicit region, logging, retention, and access-control decisions; treating provider selection alone as sufficient is risky.
warn_nfr.message: genai-inference--vertex-ai: spec indicates PII handling. Verify Vertex AI region, logging, retention, and IAM settings before treating this as compliant.
